ssz_engine_api_updates.highlight: SSZ model being added to existing execution fixtures; hash verification to confirm no regressions before release

Minimal list of non-engine methods to expose on engine port emerging: eth_syncing, eth_getBlockByNumber, eth_getBlockByHash, eth_chainId (Prysm omits eth_syncing but not opposed; Lighthouse adds eth_syncing + eth_getBlockByNumber; Nimbus adds eth_chainId + eth_syncing)

top_up_sync.highlight: Top-up sync direction discussed: whether to formalize as EIP + PFI, or simply add the engine endpoint; no decision reached
Core question: endpoint should expose whether EL has state (not just blocks) at a given block number; security model differs without state
Real-world validation: Nimbus EL bug on devnet 7 (no peers) resolved because Teku fed blocks in order — accidental demonstration of top-up sync value

http2_vs_http1.highlight: HTTP/2 required in SSZ Engine API spec for head-of-line blocking avoidance; Nimbus libraries do not currently support HTTP/2
Dustin questions whether HTTP/2 benefit is empirically validated for Engine API's inherently serial, stateful request pattern vs. web browser use case
Consensus: benchmark HTTP/2 vs HTTP/1.1 for Engine API workload before mandating HTTP/2; raise in #ssz channel

organizational.highlight: Call time proposed to move ~4–6 hours earlier to accommodate EU/Asia participants and allow Marius to join

action_items.action: Run HTTP/2 vs HTTP/1.1 benchmarks for Engine API workload and post results to #ssz channel
action_items.owner: All teams (Nethermind volunteered; Tamaghna to coordinate if no one picks it up)

action_items.action: Poll Marius and attendees for better call time; move recurring slot ~4–6 hours earlier

decisions.decision: Provisional minimal non-engine method list for engine port: eth_syncing, eth_getBlockByNumber, eth_getBlockByHash, eth_chainId — to be finalized in a future call after remaining client input
